color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; font-size: 16px ; white-space: normal ; background-color: rgb(255 , 242 , 204)">Closure means that an inner function always has access to the vars and parameters of its outer function, even after the outer function has returned.</i>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="630" x="400" y="120" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-3" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e1d5e7;strokeColor=#9673a6;" value="<font style="font-size: 16px">nestedFunction</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="110" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-4" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e1d5e7;strokeColor=#9673A6;align=left;" value="<span style="background-color: rgb(230 , 208 , 222) ; font-size: 15px"><span style="color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal">You have learned that we can create&nbsp;</span><a href="https://www.tutorialsteacher.com/javascript/javascript-function" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 123 , 255) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal">nested functions</a><span style="color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal">&nbsp;in JavaScript. Inner function can access variables and parameters of an outer function (however, cannot access arguments object of outer function). Consider the following example.</span></span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="140" width="320" y="320" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-5" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e1d5e7;strokeColor=#9673A6;" value="<pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: 15px ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px ; text-align: justify"><code style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: inherit ; color: inherit ; word-break: normal ; padding: 0px ; white-space: inherit ; line-height: 1rem ; background-color: rgb(230 , 208 , 222)"><b><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> OuterFunction() {<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> outerVariable = 1;<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> InnerFunction() {<br> alert(outerVariable);<br> }<br><br> InnerFunction();<br>}</b></code></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="190" width="320" y="460" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-6" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;strokeColor=#9673a6;fillColor=#e1d5e7;align=left;" value="<span style="color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; font-size: 16px ; white-space: normal ; background-color: rgb(230 , 208 , 222)">In the above example, InnerFunction() can access outerVariable.</span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="320" y="650" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-7" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;align=left;" value="<span style="color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; text-align: justify ; background-color: rgb(248 , 206 , 204)"><font style="font-size: 15px">Now, as per the definition above, InnerFunction() can access outerVariable even if it will be executed separately. Consider the following example.</font></span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="320" x="380" y="320" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-8" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" value="<pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: 15px ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px ; text-align: justify"><pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px"><code style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: inherit ; color: inherit ; word-break: normal ; padding: 0px ; white-space: inherit ; line-height: 1rem ; background-color: rgb(248 , 206 , 204)"><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> OuterFunction() {<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> outerVariable = 100;<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> InnerFunction() {<br> alert(outerVariable);<br> }<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">return</span> InnerFunction;<br>}<br><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> innerFunc = OuterFunction();<br><br>innerFunc(); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 100</span></code></pre></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="260" width="320" x="380" y="400" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-9" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;strokeColor=#b85450;fillColor=#f8cecc;align=left;" value="<div><span style="background-color: rgb(248 , 206 , 204)"><span style="font-size: 15px ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif">In the above example,&nbsp;</span><code style="font-size: 15px ; box-sizing: border-box ; word-break: break-word ; border: 1px solid rgb(175 , 178 , 181) ; padding: 0px 5px ; border-radius: 3px ; white-space: nowrap">return InnerFunction;</code><span style="font-size: 15px ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif">&nbsp;returns InnerFunction from OuterFunction when you call OuterFunction(). A variable&nbsp;</span><code style="font-size: 15px ; box-sizing: border-box ; word-break: break-word ; border: 1px solid rgb(175 , 178 , 181) ; padding: 0px 5px ; border-radius: 3px ; white-space: nowrap">innerFunc&nbsp;</code><span style="font-size: 15px ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif">reference the InnerFunction() only, not the OuterFunction(). So now, when you call innerFunc(), it can still access&nbsp;</span><code style="font-size: 15px ; box-sizing: border-box ; word-break: break-word ; border: 1px solid rgb(175 , 178 , 181) ; padding: 0px 5px ; border-radius: 3px ; white-space: nowrap">outerVariable</code><span style="font-size: 15px ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif">&nbsp;which is declared in OuterFunction(). This is called Closure.</span></span></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="200" width="320" x="380" y="660" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-10" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#f8cecc;strokeColor=#b85450;" value="<font style="font-size: 16px">Closures</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="520" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-11" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" value="<font style="font-size: 16px">Closures Example</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="170" x="840" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-12" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;align=left;" value="<pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: 15px ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px ; text-align: justify"><code style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: inherit ; color: inherit ; word-break: normal ; padding: 0px ; white-space: inherit ; line-height: 1rem ; background-color: rgb(218 , 232 , 252)"><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> Counter() {<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> counter = 0;<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> IncreaseCounter() {<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">return</span> counter += 1;<br> };<br><br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">return</span> IncreaseCounter;<br>}<br><br><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> counter = Counter();<br>alert(counter()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 1</span><br>alert(counter()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 2</span><br>alert(counter()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 3</span><br>alert(counter()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 4</span></code></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="300" width="320" x="750" y="320" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-13" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" value="<pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px ; text-align: justify"><pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px"><span style="color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal ; background-color: rgb(218 , 232 , 252)"><font style="font-size: 15px">In the above example, outer function Counter returns the reference of inner function IncreaseCounter(). IncreaseCounter increases the outer variable counter to one. So calling inner function multiple time will increase the counter to one each time.</font></span><span style="background-color: rgb(255 , 255 , 255) ; font-size: 15px"><br></span></pre></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="320" x="750" y="620" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-15" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;gradientColor=#ffd966;" value="<span style="font-size: 16px">When to use Closures</span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="170" x="1170" y="240" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="MpZk_CsKFq6REEgX6KjP-16" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;align=left;gradientColor=#ffd966;" value="<pre class="csharpcode" style="box-sizing: border-box ; font-family: &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: 15px ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; background-position: 0px 0px ; background-repeat: repeat ; background-attachment: scroll ; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px"><p style="box-sizing: border-box ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 1rem ; line-height: 1.9rem ; overflow-wrap: break-word ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal"><span style="background-color: rgb(255 , 229 , 153)">Closure is useful in hiding implementation detail in JavaScript. In other words, it can be useful to create private variables or functions.</span></p><p style="box-sizing: border-box ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 1rem ; line-height: 1.9rem ; overflow-wrap: break-word ; color: rgb(24 , 23 , 23) ; font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal"><span style="background-color: rgb(255 , 229 , 153)">The following example shows how to create private functions &amp; variable.</span></p></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="220" width="345" x="1115" y="320" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell &#34;consolas&#34; , &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: 15px ; margin-top: 0px ; margin-bottom: 0px ; overflow: auto ; border: 0px ; background-image: none ; &#34;courier new&#34; , &#34;courier&#34; , monospace ; font-size: inherit ; color: inherit ; word-break: normal ; padding: 0px ; white-space: inherit ; line-height: 1rem ; background-color: rgb(255 , 229 , 153)"><span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> counter = (<span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span>() {<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">var</span> privateCounter = 0;<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span> changeBy(val) {<br> privateCounter += val;<br> }<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">return</span> {<br> increment: <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span>() {<br> changeBy(1);<br> },<br> decrement: <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span>() {<br> changeBy(-1);<br> },<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">value</span>: <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">function</span>() {<br> <span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">return</span> privateCounter;<br> }<br> }; <br>})();<br><br>alert(counter.<span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">value</span>()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 0</span><br>counter.increment();<br>counter.increment();<br>alert(counter.<span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">value</span>()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 2</span><br>counter.decrement();<br>alert(counter.<span class="kwrd" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(0 , 0 , 255)">value</span>()); <span class="rem" style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: green">// 1</span></code></pre></pre></pre>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="510" width="345" x="1115" y="540" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell font-family: &#34;verdana&#34; , &#34;geneva&#34; , sans-serif ; white-space: normal ; background-color: rgb(255 , 229 , 153)"><font style="font-size: 14px">In the above example, increment(), decrement() and value() becomes public function because they are included in the return object, whereas changeBy() function becomes private function because it is not returned and only used internally i.style: box-sizing: border-box; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; white-space: normal; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 242, 204), rgb(40, 29, 0));

div.i: Closure means that an inner function always has access to the vars and parameters of its outer function, even after the outer function has returned.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 118px; height: 1px; padding-top: 220px; margin-left: 111px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 318px; height: 1px; padding-top: 350px; margin-left: 2px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal;

span.span: You have learned that we can create

a.style: box-sizing: border-box; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 123, 255), rgb(54, 160, 255)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal;

span.span: in JavaScript. Inner function can access variables and parameters of an outer function (however, cannot access arguments object of outer function). Consider the following example.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 318px; height: 1px; padding-top: 515px; margin-left: 1px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

pre.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; font-size: 15px; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; overflow: auto; border: 0px light-dark(currentcolor, rgb(237, 237, 237)); background-image: none; background-position: 0px 0px; background-repeat: repeat; background-attachment: scroll; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px; text-align: justify;

code.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; font-size: inherit; color: inherit; word-break: normal; padding: 0px; white-space: inherit; line-height: 1rem; background-color: light-dark(rgb(230, 208, 222), rgb(67, 48, 60));

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; white-space: normal; background-color: light-dark(rgb(230, 208, 222), rgb(67, 48, 60));

div.span: In the above example, InnerFunction() can access outerVariable.

rect.style: fill: light-dark(rgb(248, 206, 204), rgb(81, 45, 43)); stroke: light-dark(rgb(184, 84, 80), rgb(215, 129, 126));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 318px; height: 1px; padding-top: 320px; margin-left: 382px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;

span.font: Now, as per the definition above, InnerFunction() can access outerVariable even if it will be executed separately. Consider the following example.

span.span: returns InnerFunction from OuterFunction when you call OuterFunction(). A variable

code.style: font-size: 15px; box-sizing: border-box; word-break: break-word; border: 1px solid light-dark(rgb(175, 178, 181), rgb(82, 85, 88)); padding: 0px 5px; border-radius: 3px; white-space: nowrap;

span.style: font-size: 15px; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif;

span.span: reference the InnerFunction() only, not the OuterFunction(). So now, when you call innerFunc(), it can still access

code.style: font-size: 15px; box-sizing: border-box; word-break: break-word; border: 1px solid light-dark(rgb(175, 178, 181), rgb(82, 85, 88)); padding: 0px 5px; border-radius: 3px; white-space: nowrap;

span.style: font-size: 15px; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif;

span.span: which is declared in OuterFunction(). This is called Closure.

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal; background-color: light-dark(rgb(218, 232, 252), rgb(29, 41, 59));
span.font: In the above example, outer function Counter returns the reference of inner function IncreaseCounter(). IncreaseCounter increases the outer variable counter to one. So calling inner function multiple time will increase the counter to one each time.

display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

pre.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; font-size: 15px; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; overflow: auto; border: 0px light-dark(currentcolor, rgb(237, 237, 237)); background-image: none; background-position: 0px 0px; background-repeat: repeat; background-attachment: scroll; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px;

p.style: box-sizing: border-box; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1rem; line-height: 1.9rem; overflow-wrap: break-word; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal;
p.span: Closure is useful in hiding implementation detail in JavaScript. In other words, it can be useful to create private variables or functions.

p.style: box-sizing: border-box; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 1rem; line-height: 1.9rem; overflow-wrap: break-word; color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal;
p.span: The following example shows how to create private functions & variable.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 343px; height: 1px; padding-top: 755px; margin-left: 1116px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

pre.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; overflow: auto; border: 0px light-dark(currentcolor, rgb(237, 237, 237)); background-image: none; background-position: 0px 0px; background-repeat: repeat; background-attachment: scroll; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px; text-align: justify;

box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; overflow: auto; border: 0px light-dark(currentcolor, rgb(237, 237, 237)); background-image: none; background-position: 0px 0px; background-repeat: repeat; background-attachment: scroll; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px;

box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; font-size: 15px; margin-top: 0px; margin-bottom: 0px; overflow: auto; border: 0px light-dark(currentcolor, rgb(237, 237, 237)); background-image: none; background-position: 0px 0px; background-repeat: repeat; background-attachment: scroll; padding: 10px 0px 10px 5px;

code.style: box-sizing: border-box; font-family: consolas, "courier new", courier, monospace; font-size: inherit; color: inherit; word-break: normal; padding: 0px; white-space: inherit; line-height: 1rem; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 229, 153), rgb(62, 40, 0));

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 338px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1075px; margin-left: 1117px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(24, 23, 23), rgb(217, 217, 217)); font-family: verdana, geneva, sans-serif; white-space: normal; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 229, 153), rgb(62, 40, 0));
span.font: In the above example, increment(), decrement() and value() becomes public function because they are included in the return object, whereas changeBy() function becomes private function because it is not returned and only used internally by increment() and decrement().
